channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: African fabrics that define culture and style beyond Adire and Aso Oke
item.description: This Guardian Life feature explores a rich panorama of African textiles that extend beyond the well-known Adire and Aso Oke. The piece traces how fabrics such as Aso Oke (Yoruba handwoven cloth linked...

item.title: ITLOS rules Equatorial Guinea violated maritime law in Heroic Idun detention, bringing relief to Kochi sailor
item.description: Kochi-based sailor Sanu Jose, who served as chief officer of the cargo vessel MT Heroic Idun, has expressed deep relief after an international tribunal ruled against Equatorial Guinea over the ship’s ...

item.title: White House denies report claiming advisers urged JD Vance to reduce social media activity
item.description: The White House strongly denied a New York Times report alleging that Vice President JD Vance had been advised by senior officials, including Chief of Staff Susie Wiles, to take a step back from his s...

item.title: <non-clickbait, descriptive title>
item.description: <a rewritten summary between 200 and 500 words highlights>

item.title: Tanzania, South Korea Sign Deal to Upgrade Railway Network
item.description: Tanzania na South Korea wameshirikiana rasmi kwa lengo la kuimarisha miundombinu ya reli nchini Tanzania. Hii partnership ni hatua kubwa ya kukuza uhusiano wa kiuchumi kati ya nchi hizi mbili. Reli za...

item.title: Ken Paxton faces public scrutiny over past courthouse incident as divorce trial approaches
item.description: Texas Attorney General-turned-Senate candidate Ken Paxton is set to appear at his upcoming divorce trial, attracting renewed attention due to a previous incident at the same courthouse. In 2013, Paxto...

item.title: Study highlights border and visa challenges faced by travellers with weaker passports
item.description: The article examines the difficulties faced by international tourists holding passports that rank low on global mobility indices. Based on research by Isabella Qing Ye and co-author Samira Zare, it hi...

item.title: Montreal Victoire Celebrate First PWHL Championship with Downtown Parade
item.description: After a season filled with challenges, the Montreal Victoire have proudly claimed their first Professional Women’s Hockey League (PWHL) championship, defeating the Ottawa Charge 4-0 on May 20 to becom...

item.title: Lewis Group implements turnaround strategy for underperforming UFO furniture division
item.description: Lewis Group is working hard to turn around its UFO furniture chain, which has been struggling since it was acquired in 2018 for R320 million. CEO Johan Enslin admitted that the division had another to...

item.title: Paris Saint-Germain Win 2026 Champions League After Penalty Shootout Against Arsenal
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) don carry the 2026 UEFA Champions League trophy after dem beat Arsenal 4-3 on penalties following one tensed 1-1 draw inside extra time at Puskas Arena for Budapest, Hungary....

item.title: PSG retain Champions League title after penalty shootout win over Arsenal
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain don win UEFA Champions League again after dem beat Arsenal for final wey end 1-1 after 120 minutes and later go into penalty shootout. The match happen on Saturday and e no be easy...

item.title: How karate training in Sydney dojos is helping teachers and students build resilience and mental wellbeing
item.description: In a suburban Sydney dojo, karate is being reframed as more than a martial art focused on physical strength, with students and instructors highlighting its role in supporting mental wellbeing and emot...

item.title: Concerns raised over Queensland’s slowdown in single-use plastic bans
item.description: Queensland’s progress on phasing out single-use plastics has been criticised by environmental campaigners, who say the state has effectively stalled its reform agenda. While Queensland introduced earl...

item.title: PTI signals possible withdrawal from political system amid concerns over fair electoral space
item.description: Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) senior leader and former National Assembly Speaker Asad Qaiser has indicated that the party is seriously thinking about exiting the current political system, citing cont...

item.title: South African Furniture Industry Shows Signs of Stabilisation Amid Recovery Efforts
item.description: South Africa’s furniture manufacturing industry is showing early signs of stabilisation after more than two decades of decline. Employment in the sector has stabilised at just over 30,000, down from a...

item.title: EALA to convene special sitting to consider $110.9 million EAC budget for 2026/27 financial year
item.description: The East African Legislative Assembly (EALA) is scheduled to hold a special virtual sitting on Monday, June 1, 2026, to deliberate on the East African Community (EAC) budget estimates for the 2026/27 ...

item.title: DepEd and DOLE to deploy 240,000 TUPAD workers for school preparation under Brigada Eskwela 2026
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) and the Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE) are set to mobilize around 240,000 beneficiaries of the Tulong Panghanapbuhay sa Ating Disadvantaged/Displaced Wor...

item.title: Everyman cinema faces challenges as it aims to stabilise and grow
item.description: Everyman, the UK-based luxury cinema chain, is facing a period of financial and operational difficulty after years of growth and acclaim. Known for its plush seating and gourmet offerings, including p...

item.title: Graham Platner Faces Scrutiny Over Explicit Texts Revealed by Wife
item.description: Democratic U.S. Senate candidate Graham Platner of Maine is facing increased scrutiny after reports revealed that his wife, Amy Gertner, discovered sexually explicit text messages on his phone. Accord...

item.title: Cleophas Malala announces 2027 Kakamega gubernatorial bid after 2022 election defeat
item.description: Cleophas Malala, the deputy leader of the Democracy for the Citizens Party (DCP), has officially declared his intention to contest the Kakamega gubernatorial seat in the 2027 general elections. Speaki...
